seed_calibration.seed_foundational_gap: hand-waved sparkline contract, lone single-source mockup, eyebrow-only source rule under multi-source bursts, freshest double-encoded by gutter+bold, verb-glyph dictionary open, codepoint widths not pinned, unfocus rules deferred

hud-fidelity.rationale: Six of the seven seed gaps close cleanly under this lens. (1) §Sparkline contract (lines 134–148) pins all six dimensions — geometry (8 cells × 8 s buckets = 64 s window), fixed alphabet U+2581–U+2588, auto-rescale floor at 1, 1 Hz refresh, three explicit empty states, unfocus slide — and explicitly retires the seed's '7/min' suffix as 'the panel re-stating itself, the canonical Tufte data-ink offence' (line 150). That is the right reflex applied to the right cell. (2) §Verb-glyph column (lines 157–199) closes the dictionary at exactly five entries with codepoints and rationale (eye/list/format-list-bulleted/magnify/pencil/play) and pins a four-rule precedence so column 1 always has a deterministic content rule, not a render-time guess. (3) §Glyph palette (lines 376–404) retires the seed's EAW-Ambiguous '●' (U+25CF), '✓'/'✗' (U+2713/U+2717) and EAW-Wide '⚠' (U+26A0) and replaces them with PUA codepoints (U+F0000–U+F1AFF) explicitly stated as EAW Neutral / single column-cell — the small-multiples instrument can no longer break alignment across locales. (4) Bold-overuse is reduced: freshest description drops from Tier 1 (bold) to Tier 2 (secondary, line 318) — bold is no longer reflex weight. Two real demerits keep this off 10. (a) Freshest is still double-encoded — gutter '●' (binary, freshest only) PLUS description Tier-2-vs-Tier-3 (binary, freshest vs older). The candidate explicitly rejects a recency-rank gradient gutter on exactly this ground in §Open Q2 (lines 419–422: 'rejected as double-encoding — description Tier 2 → 3 already encodes recency') without noticing the same logic indicts its own kept double-encoding; one of the two should retire. (b) The sparkline empty-state (i) 'no-logs' (line 146) suppresses the row entirely — but the rubric explicitly rewards 'sparkline always rendering meaningful shape,' and case (ii) window-empty already does the right thing (flat '▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁'); the no-logs case should be unified, not special-cased. Minor: §Verb-glyph Rule 4 (line 192) renders 'single space' for unclassified events; the defence in lines 194–199 ('gaps are informative') is reasonable but a baseline glyph would close the column the way ▁ closes the sparkline.

four-questions.rationale: All four are now answered by glance with peripherally-perceivable shape, not text. Liveness: sparkline existence + auto-rescaled non-flat shape (single primitive, single line). Pattern: sparkline silhouette with the floor-at-1 rule preventing single-event saturation — the seed's stuck/idle conflation is partially addressed by the explicit window-empty case (case ii, flat baseline = 'window has no events but agent emitted earlier'). Exception: this is the dramatic improvement from seed. Column-1 SEV_ERROR displacement (§Verb-glyph rule 1, line 185) means failed rows show the existing severity glyph in the verb position — under load, a vertical red stripe accumulates in column 1 (State 4 mockup, lines 348–353), which is the canonical 'gutter mass → exception count by glance' move the seed left on the table. The single surviving verb glyph in a stripe of '✗' (line 362–363) is itself diagnostic — that is shape-as-meaning. Latest: gutter '●' (nf-md-record, U+F05CB) on freshest row (eyebrow mode) plus description Tier-2 weight; in chip mode (lines 233–239) the gutter is forfeit and latest reads only by position + Tier-step, which is thinner but still glance-readable. Two residual weaknesses keep this off 10. (a) Liveness is unrepresented in the no-logs empty state (sparkline suppressed) — the user must read the text '(no recent activity)' to know the band is alive; the seed-equivalent flaw survives in this one case. (b) The verb-glyph column shifts position across modes — col 1 in eyebrow mode (State 2), col 4 in chip mode (State 3 layout, lines 244–252) — so verb-shape cannot be read across a mode transition; within a source-run the column is stable, which is the only reason this is a 9 not an 8.

terminal-feasibility.rationale: All glyphs are attainable, all widths are pinned, all four states are mocked. (1) Glyph budget: exactly 6 new entries — 5 verb glyphs + nf-md-record gutter (lines 385–390) — at the ceiling but not over, with codepoint and EAW class per glyph. The chip separator '│' (U+2502) is reused not new (line 403). SEV_ERROR reuses existing nf-md-alert-circle (line 392). (2) 30-col degradation: every mockup carries the explicit ruler-at-30 commitment in §Constraints (lines 24–26: 'all mockups in this iteration are drawn at 30 cols'). State 3 chip mode pins the column accounting (cols 0–5 for chip+verb+pad, ≤24 cells for description, lines 244–252) so the implementor knows the truncation budget. (3) All four states drawn explicitly (Empty/Single-source/Multi-source/Error-heavy, lines 287–366) — the seed's lone-single-source-mockup gap is fully closed. (4) Producer-side `verb?` tag is labelled SHOULD with a renderer-side regex-classifier fallback (lines 175–192), so the design is buildable today against the existing log shape — the four-rule precedence (error → verb → regex-classify → space) is deterministic, not aspirational. Two small lens-relevant feasibility frictions keep this off 10. (a) The sparkline 1 Hz tick (line 145) assumes 'the existing focused-session timer' — for the right-edge cell to fall off smoothly without new events, this timer needs to fire renders even when logs[] is unchanged; this is plausible but unverified. (b) The renderer-side regex-classifier is described as living in 'classify.ts' adjacent to vocab.ts (line 191) — this is architecture, not file creation, but the dependence on a regex on message strings means the column-1 small-multiples discipline is enforced from text-shape rather than from data, which the seed's phase-2 already flagged as a small-multiples threat; it's mitigated here by Rule 4 (defaults to space rather than wrong glyph), so it doesn't break the instrument, but it does mean the 'verb-shape' read is best-effort until the producer ships `verb?`.

asi_candidate.summary: Make the sparkline a closed instrument: render flat '▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁' in the no-logs empty state, the same way §Sparkline contract case (ii) already renders it for window-empty. The band's top row never disappears.
asi_candidate.actionable_change: In §Sparkline contract (line 146), unify empty-state cases (i) and (ii): both render flat '▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁' across all eight cells. Drop the 'sparkline row suppressed entirely' branch; the no-logs case becomes the most-extreme version of window-empty, not a special case. Update §State 1 — empty (lines 287–294) to show the flat baseline at the top of the band, then the air row, then '(no recent activity)' beneath in Tier 4 (or drop the text entirely — the flat shape carries 'nothing happening' as cleanly as the phrase, and this is exactly the Tufte 'shape replaces text' move). Add one line to §Sparkline contract under Empty states stating the rule: 'the sparkline is a closed instrument — its eight cells render in every state of the band, including no-logs. Calm reads as a continuous flat line, not as absence of the band.' The contract already does the work for case (ii); extending to case (i) is one clause and one mockup change, no glyph-budget impact, no producer-side change.
asi_candidate.why_this_lens: The judge brief's information-designer lens explicitly rewards 'closed instruments (column-1 always populated, sparkline always rendering meaningful shape).' The candidate's §Sparkline contract gets five of six empty-state decisions right and then breaks the closure on case (i): when logs[] is empty, the sparkline row is suppressed and the band collapses to a text message. That is the design becoming a dialog ('no recent activity' as a sentence the user reads) rather than a HUD ('the band is alive and the agent is silent' as a shape the user perceives). The fix is internally consistent (case (ii) already does it), it strengthens the four-questions liveness answer in the one state where it currently fails, and it replaces a TEXT primitive with a SHAPE primitive — exactly what the lens scores on. Among the candidate's three remaining Tufte demerits (freshest double-encoded; sparkline suppressed in no-logs; verb-column rule-4 single-space), this is the one the rubric rewards most directly and the cheapest to land.
asi_candidate.scope: Single-section edit to docs/simmer/activity-zone/iteration-1-candidate.md: rewrite the Empty-states row of the §Sparkline contract table to unify cases (i) and (ii); rewrite §State 1 — empty to render the flat sparkline; add one prose line stating the 'closed instrument' rule. No glyph additions, no producer-side changes, no impact on the other three state mockups.

notes_for_phase_2: Secondary Tufte concern not in the ASI: freshest is still double-encoded — gutter '●' on row 0 AND description Tier-2-vs-Tier-3 binary split. The candidate explicitly rejects a recency-rank gradient gutter in §Open Q2 on the ground that 'description Tier 2 → 3 already encodes recency' without noticing the same logic applies to its kept gutter+Tier-step pairing. A single-encoding (gutter alone, OR Tier-step alone) would be tighter. Worth raising in synthesis if no other panelist flags it; lower priority than the closed-instrument fix because it is a calibration call, not a closure.

Tertiary: §Verb-glyph Rule 4 (line 192) renders 'single space' for non-tool / unclassified events. The candidate defends this in lines 194–199 ('gaps in the stripe are themselves informative'), and the defence is real — small-multiples discipline survives because the column position never moves. But a baseline glyph (analogous to ▁ as zero-mark in the sparkline) would close the column the way the sparkline closes the top row. Held back as the ASI because resolving it costs glyph budget (already at 6/6) or requires reusing an existing chevron / box-drawing primitive whose semantics would conflict.

Quaternary: the verb-glyph column position shifts across source-modes — col 1 in eyebrow mode (State 2), col 4 in chip mode (State 3). Within a source-run the column is stable, but the eye cannot read verb-shape across an eyebrow→chip→eyebrow transition. This is a small-multiples-discipline cost of the hybrid source rule; it is probably the right trade for 30-col degradation but should be acknowledged.

If another lens proposes dropping the entire empty state to a single line ('(no recent activity)' alone, no air, no sparkline), my ASI dies cleanly — the closure either ships or it doesn't; no half-measure.
